display.purpose: Largest headings and view titles; used sparingly.

body.purpose: Default reading and input text; cap prose at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Dense UI labels, event titles, controls; the calendar-grid workhorse.

script.purpose: Caveat handwriting on the Dedication only.

shadows.name: state-lift
shadows.purpose: Hover/focus and active drag/resize; signals an element is interactive or in motion. Never at rest.

shadows.name: floating-panel
shadows.purpose: Separates menus, overlays, and the command palette from the grid beneath.

motion.name: default-transition
motion.purpose: Default duration for state transitions on controls.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Entrance/acknowledge animations (account sync status, someday commit).

components.name: Priority Button
components.description: Save/action button tinted by event priority; borderless at rest, hover-reveals by shifting to the dark canvas and brightening the label.
components.css: .ds-btn-priority { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-width: 158px; padding: 8px; border: none; border-radius: 2px; background: #6f7d8a; color: #0d1017; font-family: Rubik, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.5s, color 0.55s; } .ds-btn-priority:hover { background: #0d1017; color: #9fb0bf; } .ds-btn-priority:focus-visible { outline: none; border: 2px solid hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.5); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Borderless field that reveals itself on hover; no resting outline, focus conveyed by context.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; height: 34px; padding: 0 8px; border: none; outline: none; border-radius: 4px; background: #fafafa; color: #0d1017; font-family: Rubik, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; transition: 0.3s; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: hsl(219 8% 46% / 0.9); } .ds-input:hover { background: hsl(219 18% 34% / 0.2); }

components.name: Priority Tag
components.description: Muted priority-colored chip categorizing an event; hover brightens the fill.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 4px; background: #86d0bb; color: #0d1017; font-family: Rubik, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.688rem; font-weight: 500; transition: background-color 0.3s; } .ds-tag:hover { background: #9fdcca; }

components.name: Event Block
components.description: A timed event on the calendar grid; the brightest content on the dark canvas, tinted by priority, lifted on hover.
components.css: .ds-event { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; padding: 4px 8px; border-radius: 4px; background: #6e7f8d; color: #0d1017; font-family: Rubik, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: box-shadow 0.2s, filter 0.2s; } .ds-event-time { font-size: 0.563rem; opacity: 0.75; } .ds-event-title { font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; } .ds-event:hover { box-shadow: 0 2px 8px hsla(0 0% 0% / 0.25); filter: brightness(1.08); }

components.name: Command Palette Item
components.description: A row in the keyboard-first command palette; a floating light surface above the dark canvas.
components.html:
Go to Today
Create event
Open Now mode
components.css: .ds-cmd { width: 320px; padding: 6px; border-radius: 6px; background: #fafafa; box-shadow: 0 6px 24px hsla(0 0% 0% / 0.35); font-family: Rubik, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-cmd-row { padding: 8px 10px; border-radius: 4px; color: #0d1017; font-size: 0.875rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s; } .ds-cmd-row:hover { background: hsl(219 18% 34% / 0.12); } .ds-cmd-active { background: #57c1ff; color: #0d1017; }

narrative.northStar: The Dark Calendar Canvas, One Accent
narrative.overview: Compass is a near-black blue canvas where the only truly saturated color is a single sky-blue accent (#57c1ff). Everything structural, the grid lines, panels, borders, and most text, lives in a narrow blue-gray band against a deep #0d1017 background. Color enters the screen only where it carries meaning: the accent marks selection and primary actions, and a small set of muted priority colors tints the event blocks themselves. The interface is dim and low-contrast at rest so the user's events and current task are the brightest things on screen. The one deliberately human note is handwriting: the Caveat script font on the Dedication, a personal touch at the margin of an otherwise precise instrument.
keyCharacteristics: Deep blue-black surface (#0d1017), built up by tonal layering, not boxes.
Exactly one bright accent (#57c1ff); meaning, not decoration.
Muted priority colors are the only other saturation, and only on events.
Quiet, borderless controls that reveal themselves on hover/focus.
One UI typeface (Rubik), with Caveat handwriting as a single human accent.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Exactly one saturated accent (#57c1ff) on any screen, reserved for selection, primary action, and info. A second saturated hue outside an event block or status signal is wrong.

rules.name: The Color-Means-Something Rule
rules.body: Saturation is forbidden as decoration. The accent means selected/primary/info; priority colors mean the event's category; status colors mean state. Structural chrome stays blue-gray.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Rubik is the only UI typeface. Display fonts in labels, buttons, or data are forbidden. Caveat is the single exception, on the Dedication only.

rules.name: The Layer-By-Light Rule
rules.body: Build depth by stepping surface lightness (#0d1017 to #11151c to translucent panels), not by drawing borders or dropping shadows on resting elements.

rules.name: The Shadow-Is-State Rule
rules.body: A shadow at rest is a bug. Shadows appear only on hover, focus, active drag/resize, or for layers that genuinely float above the grid.

dos: Do keep exactly one saturated accent (#57c1ff) per screen, for selection, primary action, and info only.
Do build depth by stepping surface lightness (#0d1017 to #11151c to translucent panels), per the Layer-By-Light Rule.
Do leave controls borderless at rest and reveal them via background/color shift on hover and focus.
Do reserve priority colors (Work/Relations/Self/Unassigned) for event blocks and tags, not chrome.
Do carry all UI in Rubik, using weight (400/500/700) and the fixed rem scale for hierarchy.
Do confine Caveat handwriting to the Dedication.
Do hold body/label text to WCAG AA contrast against the dark surfaces; bump muted blue-grays toward fg-primary when contrast is close.
donts: Don't rebuild the cluttered toolbars, nested menus, or busy chrome of Google/Outlook Calendar.
Don't fall into the generic shadcn look: neutral-gray cards, stock radii and shadows, the interchangeable component-library surface.
Don't ship a generic SaaS dashboard: card grids, hero-metric templates, or gradient accents standing in for design.
Don't introduce playful/consumer-cute candy colors, mascots, or heavy illustration, or enterprise navy-and-gray corporate density.
Don't add a second saturated accent, or use the accent decoratively where it carries no meaning.
Don't put a shadow on a resting element; shadows are state (hover/focus/drag) or floating layers only.
Don't use display or handwriting fonts for UI labels, buttons, or data.
Don't add resting borders to inputs or buttons; the focus border is the one intentional exception.
